spec_natural_language: function _isAuthorized MUST return false when block.timestamp is greater than or equal to expiry time (unless expiry equals AUTHORIZED constant)

violated_invariant.precondition: expiry != UNAUTHORIZED && expiry != AUTHORIZED && block.timestamp >= expiry
violated_invariant.expected_behavior: Must return false and fall through to check $.allowed[account], not return true

extraction_reasoning: The finding clearly identifies the exact line and logic flaw: the condition uses '>' instead of '<' for timestamp comparison against expiry, causing authorization to persist after expiry. The fix is explicitly shown in the recommended mitigation.
